author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
hari hari! kabe hena daśā habe mora
seviba dohāra pada ānande vibhora

bhramara hoiyā sadā rahiba caraṇe
śrī-caraṇāmṛta sadā kariba āsvādane

ei āśā kari āmi⋅yata sakhīgaṇa
tomādera kṛpāya haya vāñchita pūraṇa

bahudina vāñchā kari’ pūrṇa jāte haya
sabe meli dayā kara hoiyā sadaya

sevā āśe narottama kānde divāniśi
kṛpā kari’ kara more anugata dāsī
en_translation:
Hari Hari! When will such a state be mine? Overwhelmed with joy, I will serve the feet of the Couple.

Becoming a bee, I will always remain at the feet; I will always taste the nectar of the holy feet.

I cherish this hope—all you sakhīs! By your mercy, the desire is fulfilled.

That which I have desired for many days may be fulfilled—all of you meeting together, be kind, becoming merciful.

In the hope of service, Narottama weeps day and night; mercifully make me a follower maidservant.
